howItWorks.title: How This Capify Alternative Makes Lyric Videos

steps.title: Upload your song
steps.description: Drop an MP3, WAV, FLAC, AAC, or OGG file — or paste a link. No account needed to start, and no manual lyric typing required. Try LyricVideoMaker with the same song you used in Capify and compare the results.

steps.title: AI transcribes and syncs
steps.description: AI transcribes lyrics from the audio automatically and syncs every line to the beat — in 50+ languages. Click any line to edit. AI also reads the song's mood to generate matching visuals.

steps.title: Preview and export
steps.description: Watch the full lyric video before publishing. Compare the preview quality, lyric accuracy, and visual feel against Capify. Export in 16:9 for YouTube, 9:16 for TikTok, Reels, and Shorts, or 1:1 for feed posts. Free to export, no subscription required.

whyChoose.title: Why Test Your Lyric Video on LyricVideoMaker

items.question: Is LyricVideoMaker free as a Capify alternative?
items.answer: Yes. LyricVideoMaker is free to use as a Capify alternative — free credits to start, export included, no subscription or credit card.

items.question: How is LyricVideoMaker different from Capify?
items.answer: LyricVideoMaker auto-transcribes lyrics from audio, syncs every line to the beat, generates mood-driven AI visuals, and exports in multiple formats — all from one upload.

items.question: Do I need to type lyrics like in Capify?
items.answer: No — unlike a type-it-yourself workflow, AI transcribes every lyric line from your audio in 50+ languages. You only review and edit.

items.question: Can I export for TikTok and YouTube?
items.answer: Yes. Export in 16:9 for YouTube, 9:16 for TikTok, Reels, and Shorts, or 1:1 for feed posts. All formats from one upload.

items.question: Can I use exported lyric videos commercially?
items.answer: Yes. Your exports are yours to monetize and release — the same freedom you'd expect from Capify. Just make sure you have the rights to the music you upload.

items.question: How hard is it to switch from Capify?
items.answer: There's nothing to migrate. Upload the same song you used in Capify and you'll have a finished lyric video in a couple of minutes — no project files to move, no setup.

contentSections.heading: Capify and LyricVideoMaker: same goal, different approach
body: Capify and LyricVideoMaker are after the same outcome — turning a song into a lyric video without a manual editing project. Both lean on AI, so the real question is not whether they automate the work, but how good the result is on your actual track: lyric accuracy, beat timing, readable layout, and visuals that fit the song.
That makes this an unusually direct comparison. Run the same song through both, then judge the transcript, the sync, and the look side by side instead of comparing feature lists on paper.

contentSections.heading: When Capify might be the better fit
body: If you already have a Capify workflow you are happy with, your team is trained on it, and it produces results you like for your genre, there is little reason to switch for its own sake. Sticking with a tool your process is built around has real value.

The fair way to frame it: Capify is a capable option, and the goal here is not to claim it is bad — it is to give you a free, like-for-like result to compare against so the decision is based on output, not marketing.

contentSections.heading: When LyricVideoMaker wins
body: LyricVideoMaker focuses on the parts that decide whether a lyric video actually works: transcription you can trust and correct, beat-accurate timing, mood-driven visuals generated from the song, and clean multi-format export — all from one upload, free to start.
If your current result feels generic, the lyrics drift off the beat, or the words get lost behind the visuals, those are exactly the areas this tool is built to get right.

contentSections.heading: Switching from Capify takes one upload
body: There is nothing to migrate. Upload the same song you used in Capify and you will have a finished lyric video to compare in a couple of minutes — no project files to move, no setup, no learning curve before you can judge the result.
Because the free plan exports real videos, you can A/B the two without committing money or time up front.

contentSections.heading: The honest test: same song, side by side
body: The most reliable comparison is practical, not theoretical. Use the exact track you already tested in Capify, generate it here, and inspect four things: are the lyrics correct, do lines land on the beat, is the text readable across verse and chorus, and do the visuals feel specific to the song?
Whichever tool makes the words easiest to follow on the moments that matter is the right one for that track.

contentSections.heading: What to look for in an AI lyric video maker
body: Beyond the first impression, weigh the things that matter over many videos: editability when the AI mishears a word, consistency of timing on fast sections, export formats for every platform, and pricing that fits how often you publish.
A good AI lyric video maker should remove the chores — typing, timing, formatting — while leaving you in control of wording and look. That balance, more than any single feature, is what to compare.
